response: finding the solution requires finding the vertices of the feasible region defined by the inequalities. finding the maximum value of the objective function involves evaluating $z$ at each vertex. The vertices are $(1,0)$, $(1,2)$, and $(0,1)$. Evaluating $z$ at $(1,0)$ gives $2$. Evaluating $z$ at $(1,2)$ gives $8$. Evaluating $z$ at $(0,1)$ gives $3$. The maximum value is clearly $8$.
